pages.raw_text:
By agreement of counsel, the
two suits to break the plumbers
trust and dissolve the alleged I'M
lawful relations existing between
the master plumbers and the union
5ourneymen have been set for con-
sideration for next Tuesday morn
ing in Judge Hinkle's court. The
temporary injunction granted will
continue in force until that time.


An argument in behalf of the
dissolution of the temporary re-
straining order issued against the
master plumbers association and
the plumbers union has already
been made by Attorney Fred Mill
er In the suit brought against these
bodies by John Sarginson, plumb
ing contractor and former vice
president of the plumbers associa-
tion.


1t was held by Miller that Sar-
ginson was one of the founders of
the master plumbers association,
and such being the case he has
now no legal right to go into court
and seek relief from an alleged un.
lawful condition that he helped to
create. 1t is contended also that
the union plumbers have the right
to work for whomsoever they
please and that therefore 1t is not
binding on them legally to accept
employment with a non-association
plumber If they elect not to accept
such employment.


Attorney Winchester. on behalf
OF Sarginson, will reply to Miller
next Tuesday.
